discrimination.lifecycle_stage: Design / conceptual soundness; ongoing outcomes analysis
discrimination.expectation: The model meaningfully separates fraud from non-fraud and that separation is demonstrated, not assumed.

calibration.expectation: Predicted fraud probabilities correspond to observed frequencies, so scores can support consistent decisioning.

stability.expectation: Input/score distributions are monitored for drift, with thresholds that trigger investigation or revalidation.

fairness.expectation: Adverse outcomes (fraud flags) do not fall disproportionately on protected groups without justification.

explainability.expectation: Individual decisions can be explained in terms a validator and an adversely-affected customer could follow.

stability_of_concept_under_adversary.expectation: Because fraud is adversarial and non-stationary, performance degradation between reference and recent data is surfaced explicitly.

disclaimer: Decision-support only; not regulatory advice. Validate against the official OSFI E-23 text and institutional policy.

notes.fairness_proxy: Track-B fairness uses `region` as a proxy slice; it is NOT a protected-attribute guarantee. Interpret as a coverage check across source populations.
notes.explanation_coverage: Defaults to 0.0 unless your score_fn reports per-decision explanations; set accordingly.
